_note: Philadelphia HQ data — Lincoln Financial Field, South Philadelphia. 5 group stage matches.

venue.name: Lincoln Financial Field
venue.address: 1 Lincoln Financial Field Way, Philadelphia, PA 19148
venue.notes: Configured to 67,000+ for World Cup. Clear bag policy. No re-entry. Gates open 2.5 hours before kickoff.
venue.parkingNote: South Philly Sports Complex lots open 4 hours before kickoff. $50–$70 via official app. Walking distance parking at stadium complex shared with Citizens Bank Park and Wells Fargo Center.

transit.recommended: SEPTA Broad Street Line — Pattison Station (direct to South Philly sports complex)
lines: SEPTA Broad Street Line (Orange Line) to Pattison Station
tips: Broad Street Line is the definitive match-day option. Trains run frequently and stop 2 blocks from the stadium.
Trains from City Hall station take ~12 minutes. Board with Philly Transit Pass or cash fare.
Avoid driving — I-95 and Broad St both back up badly. No reason to drive when Broad Street Line is this easy.
Post-match: trains fill fast. Walk toward City Hall station if the Pattison queue is long.
transit.parking: South Philly parking complex lots. $50–$70 reserved, cash lots $30 walk-up. Lots fill 90+ min before kickoff.
transit.rideshare: Designated pickup on Pattison Ave east side, near Citizens Bank Park. Surge pricing expected post-match.

fanZones.name: FIFA Fan Festival — Philadelphia Museum of Art
fanZones.notes: Free entry. Rocky steps backdrop, live entertainment, international food market. Philadelphia's cultural landmark as fan zone.

fanZones.name: South Street Watch Parties
fanZones.notes: South Street bars set up outdoor screens on match days. Walking distance to Lincoln Financial via Broad St.

preGame.name: South Philadelphia Italian Market area
preGame.notes: 9th Street corridor has bars and restaurants that fill up with Eagles — and now soccer — fans. Authentic South Philly experience.

preGame.name: Xfinity Live!
preGame.notes: Massive sports bar complex directly adjacent to the stadium. Four-story venue, multiple screens. Gets extremely crowded — arrive 2+ hours early.

preGame.name: East Passyunk Ave
preGame.notes: Philly's best dining and bar street. More relaxed pre-game option than Xfinity Live. BYOB culture, excellent food.

cityEnergyForecast.crowdNote: Côte d'Ivoire vs Ecuador — Philly's opener. Strong Ivorian and Ecuadorian diaspora communities in the Northeast. Lincoln Financial sets the tournament tone for the city.
Brazil vs Haiti — Philly's Haitian community is one of the most passionate in the US. Brazil traveling support adds carnival atmosphere. One of Philly's biggest match-day moments.

France vs Iraq — Les Bleus bring massive prestige and traveling support. Philly's Franco-American community will be present. Iraq fans in the US primarily based in Detroit and Chicago but will travel.
Curaçao vs Côte d'Ivoire — Curaçao's Caribbean diaspora plus Ivorian fans make for a colorful crowd. Rematch energy from wc26-B4.
Croatia vs Ghana — Group L finale. Philly's Croatian community is strong. Ghanaian fans in the Northeast add Black Stars energy. High-stakes group stage finale.

transitPain.label: Moderate
transitPain.note: Côte d'Ivoire vs Ecuador — Sunday evening match. I-95 manageable by kickoff. Broad Street Line runs smoothly.
tips: Broad Street Line from City Hall: 12 min, trains run every 8–10 min on event days
Evening kickoff avoids rush hour — plan to arrive by 6pm
Parking at the sports complex available but reserve in advance

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: Brazil vs Haiti — Philly's biggest match-day crowd. Haitian and Brazilian communities pack public transit and streets. Late kickoff adds night energy.
tips: Broad Street Line is essential — do not drive to this match
Arrive at City Hall station 45 min before departure — trains fill fast
Haitian and Brazilian communities celebrate loudly — South Street will be electric until 2am

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: France vs Iraq — Les Bleus bring large traveling support. Evening match, transit pressure peaks post-game.
tips: French fans fill Rittenhouse and Old City bars from early afternoon
Broad Street Line is the move — skip I-95 entirely
Post-match Philly bar scene will be in full celebration or commiseration mode

transitPain.label: Low
transitPain.note: Curaçao vs Côte d'Ivoire — most manageable match-day logistics in Philly's schedule. Afternoon kickoff, smaller crowd.
tips: Best day for first-time Lincoln Financial visitors — easy logistics
Broad Street Line runs uncrowded by post-match departure
Explore East Passyunk before the match for the most relaxed Philly pre-game experience

transitPain.label: High
transitPain.note: Croatia vs Ghana — Group L finale, final weekend of group stage. Lincoln Financial at capacity, transit pressure significant.
tips: Group stage final weekend — plan the full afternoon around the match
Broad Street Line reaches capacity before post-match — board quickly
Croatian community in South Philly will start gathering from noon — great pre-match walk

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Côte d'Ivoire vs Ecuador opens Philly's World Cup. Northeast diaspora communities for both nations make for a charged atmosphere at Lincoln Financial. Good value and an accessible intro to the tournament.

shouldIGoScores.label: Don't miss it
shouldIGoScores.summary: Brazil vs Haiti in Philadelphia is one of the tournament's emotional centerpieces. Philly's Haitian community is among the most passionate in North America. Brazil's traveling support turns Lincoln Financial into a carnival. This is Philly's defining World Cup moment.

shouldIGoScores.label: Strongly Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: France vs Iraq brings international prestige to Lincoln Financial. Les Bleus are one of the most decorated traveling fan bases in the world and Philly's European contingent will be out in full.

shouldIGoScores.label: Consider
shouldIGoScores.summary: Curaçao vs Côte d'Ivoire — best ticket value in Philly's schedule. Colorful crowd, Caribbean and West African energy, easy logistics. Good day to experience Lincoln Financial without the chaos of bigger matches.

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Croatia vs Ghana — Group L finale with knockout implications. South Philly's Croatian community plus Ghanaian supporters create a charged atmosphere. Final weekend energy makes every group-stage match feel electric.
